nodes.label: Astro SEO Guide for Technical Blogs
nodes.norm_label: astro seo guide for technical blogs

links.evidence: "Canonical Site": `https://blog.nmrooms.biz.id`, the original source for all articles and metadata.
Articles live at `/blog/{slug}/` with clean, stable, non-date-based slugs.
"description": "Technical blog for Nur Mufid built with Astro."
Article table of contents and heading links are inferred from headings, not maintained in frontmatter.
Code highlighting generated during the Astro build, with only a tiny client script for copying code.
A blog article authored as `.md` or `.mdx`; Markdown is the default, MDX is available for richer articles.
Article pages do not include comments or comments placeholders in version 1.
The launch site does not include newsletter signup UI; RSS is the subscription mechanism.
a generated JSON index, not an external search service.
Light/dark presentation selected from system preference unless the reader manually persists a choice.
The site represents Nur Mufid as an individual expert, not a company publication or multi-author media site.
Prefer build-time rendering, local assets, static search
Prefer build-time rendering, local assets, static search, and no client-side framework unless an article truly needs interactivity.
Every article should have a title, description, canonical URL, publish date, updated date, tags, and category.
Prefer build-time rendering, local assets, static search
Use `BlogPosting` schema for articles and `BreadcrumbList` for article navigation.
Small manifests are easier to review, diff, and recover at 3am.
A service problem is often a selector, port, or readiness problem.
Most Kubernetes debugging starts by asking what workload owns the failing pod.
Aliases should remove typing, not hide meaning.
If you run a command twice, consider saving it in a script, Makefile, or project README.
`grep`, `find`, `xargs`, `awk`, and shell history solve more daily problems than most new tools.
